title: County Of San Diego Instructional Service Guide Ssd Commercial
text:
MINIMUM DIVERSION SERVICE LEVEL RATIO
Waste diversion means reusing, reducing, recycling or composting materials that would 
otherwise be buried in the landfill. To achieve diversion, materials need to be sorted into 
different categories. Materials lose their value when they are mixed together.
The County of San Diego’s Solid Waste Ordinance incorporates state laws regarding 
recycling (AB 341), organics recycling (AB 1826 and SB 1383), engaging customers 
(AB 827), and food donation (SB 1383). Commercial properties must separate materials 
on their premises and arrange for recycling and meet the County’s minimum diversion 
service level ratio of 35%. This means at least 35% of the weekly container capacity is 
available for recyclables and organic materials.
To review all of the County’s commercial recycling requirements, visit the County of San 
Diego website at sandiegocounty.gov.
